questions.question: Define Basis Path Testing and explain its significance in software testing.
keyPoints: Basis Path Testing is a white-box testing technique developed by Tom McCabe in 1976
Uses Control Flow Graph (CFG) to identify independent execution paths
Ensures complete branch coverage with minimum test cases
Number of basis paths equals cyclomatic complexity

questions.question: Calculate the cyclomatic complexity for a program flow graph with 12 edges and 8 nodes. How many test cases are needed?
keyPoints: Use formula V(G) = E - N + 2
Cyclomatic complexity = 6
Number of test cases = 6 (equal to cyclomatic complexity)

questions.question: What are the three methods to calculate cyclomatic complexity? Explain briefly.
keyPoints: Graph Theory Method: V(G) = E - N + 2P (edges - nodes + 2×programs)
Decision Point Method: V(G) = Number of decision points + 1
Region Method: V(G) = Number of regions in the flow graph

questions.question: Differentiate between branch coverage and path coverage in testing.
keyPoints: Branch coverage tests each decision outcome (TRUE/FALSE) at least once
Path coverage tests every unique path from entry to exit
Path coverage is stronger but may be infinite with loops
Basis path testing provides practical path coverage through independent paths

questions.question: What is a Control Flow Graph? Draw a CFG for a simple if-else statement.
keyPoints: CFG is a directed graph representing program flow
Nodes represent statements or decision points
Edges represent flow of execution between nodes
For if-else: one decision node with two branches (true/false) merging at end node

questions.question: Explain the concept of independent path in basis path testing.
keyPoints: An independent path introduces at least one new condition or statement
It must traverse at least one edge not traversed by other paths
Number of independent paths equals cyclomatic complexity
All other paths can be derived from basis paths through linear combination

questions.question: List at least four advantages of Basis Path Testing.
keyPoints: Provides structured approach to testing
Ensures complete branch coverage
Reduces number of test cases needed
Helps identify complex code sections
Objective measure of testing thoroughness
Can be automated using tools
Draw CFG with decision nodes for each condition
Node 1: Entry, Node 2: A>B decision, Node 3: B>C decision, Node 4: C>A decision
Edges connect nodes based on TRUE/FALSE outcomes
Calculate: V(G) = Number of decision points + 1 = 3 + 1 = 4
Basis paths: Path1 (T,T), Path2 (T,F), Path3 (F,T), Path4 (F,F)
Cyclomatic complexity = 4, so 4 independent paths needed

questions.question: Explain the relationship between cyclomatic complexity and software quality. How can developers use this metric to improve their code?
keyPoints: Cyclomatic complexity measures logical complexity of code
Higher complexity means more testing effort required
Complexity > 10 suggests code needs refactoring
Relationship: More decision points = more paths to test = more testing time
Developers can use it to identify risky modules, prioritize testing, simplify complex code
Best practice: Keep complexity below 10 for maintainability
Can guide code reviews and design improvements

questions.question: Explain the step-by-step procedure for performing basis path testing on a given program.
keyPoints: Step 1: Draw the Control Flow Graph (CFG) of the program
Step 2: Identify all decision points and count them
Step 3: Calculate cyclomatic complexity using V(G) = E-N+2 or decision points +1
Step 4: Identify the set of independent basis paths
Step 5: Derive test cases for each basis path
Step 6: Execute test cases and verify branch coverage
Step 7: Document results and refine if needed
The procedure ensures systematic testing approach

questions.question:
A program has the following characteristics:
- 15 edges in CFG
- 9 nodes in CFG
- 2 connected components

Calculate cyclomatic complexity and determine the minimum number of test cases required. Also explain why testing all basis paths ensures complete coverage.
keyPoints: Given: E = 15, N = 9, P = 2
Formula: V(G) = E - N + 2P
Cyclomatic complexity = 10
Minimum test cases = 10
Basis paths ensure complete branch coverage because:
- Each decision outcome is exercised through some basis path
- Independent paths cover all branches
- Cyclomatic complexity equals number of branches + 1
- Linear combination of basis paths can generate all possible paths
